reviews.review_text: This game ruined my life. Naturally I seek to spread my misfortune to others

Introduce your kid to this game.

Kid will never touch drugs — the game is digital crack.

Kid eventually becomes some kind of engineer.

Optional: occasionally check in and ask, “Engineer yet?”
(Mandatory if Asian.)

Profit.
Easily one of the best games ever, infinite replayability and is great for people with autism, my only complain would be that there's way too many unnecessary sex scenes.

- Start Factorio
- Skip Tutorial
- Think the game is too easy and mess with the settings, accidentally 10x technology costs and 10x enemy attack groups
- Die within 20 minutes
- Drop the game for a few months
- After replaying Satisfactory for the 4th time and seeing multiple Factorio Videos from DoshDoshington deciding to give Factorio another go
- Skip the Tutorial again and launch into default Freeplay
- Pushing through Oil Processing and building a Nuclear Reactor take a break from Factorio
- a few months pass and Space Age DLC releases
- Remember the fun of building the Factory and buy the DLC
- Launch into the DLC on a default settings Freeplay
- Automate Nauvis with the power of Soda and sleep deprivation 
- Launch first rocket in 40 hours
- Create Space Sience and build a Space Shuttle
- Fly to Vulcanus
- Get stuck and build a Megabase level Factory on Vulcanus
- Get annoyed from the Factory Design and restart
- Automate Nauvis and launch second rocket in under 10 hours
- Reach Vulcanus and build a small Factory for Materials and Science
- Optimize Nauvis and Fly to Fulgora
- Play for 20 hours straight and finishing Fulgora in one sitting
- Go to Gleba and Ragequit Factorio for a week because of Spoilage
- After returning start new Save
- Beat Nauvis, Vulcanus and Fulgora in 50 hours
- Return to Gleba and procceed to kill all Pentapods in 5 Biomes
- Build a Self-sustained Factory on Gleba and return to Nauvis
- Build a Cargo Hauler and a Ship to fly to Aquilo
- Get to Aquilo first try
- Build Factory with Bots on Aquilo
- Go to Nauvis and build the Final Ship to Reach the Solar System Edge 
- Reach the Solar System Edge and "win" the game
- Come back to Nauvis and Kill Bugs for 60 Hours
- Find a new purpose in live --- Kill Biters ---
- Take a weekend break from Factorio
- Return to Factorio and do some Achievments 
- Remember the Challenge I made myself at the start and retry it
- after three tries get a Basic Base and tons of Alerts 
- Get Flamethrowers
- Decide to build a Megabase
- Scaling up Science
- Nearly getting wiped out
- Rebuilding and Starting a Train based Megabase
- Securing Space
- After achieving relativ safety go to Vulcanus
- Return with lots of Science and Tungsten
- Research Artillery Wagons
- Build a 20 Locomotiv 100 Artillery Wagon Monster and 10000 Shells
- Bombard 5 Generations of Bugs
- Finally get Uranium
- Build Atomic Reactors and Nukes
- Expand the Territory controlled by the Factory
- Kill thousands of nests and Worms
- Overextending Fronts
- Getting locked into Attritional Warfare against the Biters
- Play for anotehr 300 Hours
- Kill 5 Million Biters
- Expand the Train Base into a Megabase with over 900 Trains
- Build a Megabase making hundreds of science per second

Forever locked into a perpetual War with the Biters slowly Breaking the Stalement with Quality over Quantity, but as long as the Factory grows the Bugs can't win.

So in short Factorio is a game about building a Factory and commiting War Crimes against Bugs.

And don't forget.
The Factory Must Grow
Fun game so far, can't wait to beat the game for the first time one day. Runs good on my pc.

Lets be real, I have been playing this game a week or two every few years for 10 years. I love this game, it is great. 

I am/was literally a scientist for work and still this game challenges me in ways I don't expect. Something about learning to make your first factory, then realizing you need to scrap parts of it to make a better or more efficient or just less bad version is very satisfying. You really get a sense of your progress in organization and optimization.

I bought Space Age without even ever getting to the stage in the game where that would even open up lol, because I am determined now. I play this on my Macbook Air M3, and it never ceases to amaze me how seamlessly it plays on MacOS. To have an internal mod section that works on MacOS too is such a great quality of life enhancement. To know I played this as a man in my twenties with a custom built Desktop Tower all the way into my 30s with a general laptop is just wild to me.

I recommend this game to anyone who enjoys organization, factory games, progression systems, and problem solving. Sometimes it feels like work, but for those of us who love using our minds for these types of things it is easily the best game of it's genre even to this day. I prefer it over Satisfactory, due to the isometric view.

[h1]I don't think that anyone reading a review should play Factorio.[/h1]
At this point, especially given the game's popularity, if you don't already know if Factorio is going to be for you or not, then it probably isn't. I can imagine someone getting recommended this game but then discovering that it's a complete slog for them.

And I'm going to try and articulate why.

[h3]Learning Curve? What's that?[/h3]
There's a lot of brick walls of apparent complexity that can feel incredibly daunting when you first arrive at them, and I would surmise that a large majority of people who didn't enjoy Factorio came to that conclusion at one of those moments. It's pretty common knowledge among most Factorio players that chemical science, with it's brand new buildings and fluids and oil processing and whatnot, is the first major hurdle to getting [i]invested[/i] in Factorio. I don't know how much the tutorial has advanced since the game's earlier days, but I don't even think a good tutorial would make it any more approachable.

The annoying thing is that, in isolation, nothing in Factorio is [i]that[/i] hard to understand. On the surface level, there's no singular mechanic that causes players grief. However, the amalgamation of all the systems coming together is what makes the game the beast that it can become. The biggest example I can point to of that are the ratios of inputs/outputs. There are very few [i]intuitive[/i] ratios in this game. It's not easy 1:2 or 3:4 ratios across the board, and even once you understand these ratios, there's dozens of possible bottlenecks that you have to iterate over to find any problems that come up in your factory, which can start to feel overwhelming fairly quickly.

As I play and see other games that were inspired by Factorio, they do far, far better jobs not only introducing players to their mechanics, but also making those mechanics themselves interact in a cleaner way. Shapez 2 comes to mind as a game that took everything that's satisfying about Factorio and streamlined it for a more casual audience. And for those players who want that, Factorio isn't going to cut it for you.

Factorio is not going to hold your hand at any point throughout the game, and there's a lot of people, myself included, that appreciate that aspect of it. You almost certainly will need to do "research" into Factorio to get the most out of it, and for some that's a huge dealbreaker. The question "how many green circuits should I build and where should I build them?" has a million answers and caveats that the game has little to no interest in answering for you, as that's a question you both need to be able to and willing to solve for yourself.

[h3]You, specifically, need to let go.[/h3]
If you're like me and consume a lot of Factorio content on YouTube, you'll be aware of the incredible designs people can come up with when given hundreds of hours to play this game. However, you, as a normal human person, will not get that experience. There's a reason that the top players of this game are making videos: one of the only ways to make a factory worth showing is by treating this game like a job. They're doing genuine research, planning, and deep analysis of bottlenecks and production pipelines- heck, here's a true story: I aced my Productions and Operations university-level course back when I was in college just because I'd played Factorio for over two hundred hours and understood all the concepts before we even were taught them, this game is on that kind of level.

All that to say if you're a more casual player, you're going to need to accept a lot of flaws. You're not going to have the most compact base, you're not going to be making thousands of science per minute, and you'll likely have to AFK a few times to finish up a research or two at one point. Biters will kill you, you'll have to reload saves, and you'll probably start over from the beginning multiple times. And that doesn't happen in the YouTube videos, now doesn't it!?

See, there's nothing [i]actually[/i] stopping you from making "spaghetti bases" aside from people on the internet. Even if you're cooking spaghetti, at least you're cooking something. Just look at any official marketing info from the Factorio devs, it's spaghetti all the way down. So save scum, steal blueprints off the internet, don't be afraid to outsource the parts of the game you struggle with. Stay away from trying to make perfectly optimized designs that neatly fit into grids, especially not on your first attempt. Just slap the buildings down wherever they fit and route those random belts and train lines wherever they happen to fall into place. 

Once you start doing this, I promise the game becomes 10x more enjoyable, and you'll find yourself with a far more productive factory than if you were trying to make it perfect. I tried desperately to make a beautiful, modular base, and it tanked hard. Don't be like how I was, become enlightened like I am now. /halfjoking

[h3]Get in loser, we're growing the factory.[/h3]
Honestly, the Factorio experience is one of exponential, self-driven progress that will almost always give you back what you put in tenfold. It's a game where you can never truly stop learning and growing in how you approach it, and that's not even including the bazillion mods and Space Age DLC. There's a reason so many games went on to say it was inspired by Factorio, and there's also a reason that so many of the people who played it keep coming back for more.

I don't recommend this game if you want a life. You will literally forget about the outside world, needing to eat, pee, anything.
Worth it.

Greetings,

This game is one of the most addicting games of all time. You will think about it when you sleep and it will be the first things that you think about when you wake up. The first part of this game is just about creating the basics of a factory, but afterwards you have to optimize. Then after you optimized, you realize that your optimization wasn't optimized enough. This cycle is dangerous and it will drain every second of your play time. I cannot recommend this game enough, it should be in every steam library. 

THE FACTORY MUST GROW.

That's ideal game for an engineer. Ideal way to waste thousands of hours.
If you eventually got bored playing factorio, then install some hardcore mod.

An absolutely perfect game that beautifully parallels/illustrates programming principles (pun intended). I'm genuinely afraid to get too into it as I spent 31 hours practically non stop playing it. And I know that I'm going to suddenly end up dumping 1000 hours into it like a lot of reviewers here. Unfortunately I have to work but if you're someone with a lot of time and love for resource management and building/automation then  you'll 100% love this.

its a fun time, building a factorio. i do, however, have a few complaints with this game. i think it looks a little bit yucky. even if its supposed to be a hostile environment, it could use sprucing up. maybe even some other kinds of trees. fall colors, flowers, you could even have a science machine that tells you when theres a rainbow. i also am not a fan of all the evil aliens. why couldnt i have landed somewhere with nice aliens? maybe too nice. id have to build walls. stop them from showing up at my door, singing christmas carols, messing up all my work. get out of my laboratorio. effectively its the same, from a gameplay perspective, but less hateful. enough with all the hate. another thing i think could use work is the car. i like the car. i like all cars. dont worry. however, the vehicle physics in this game are a tad lacking. personally i would like an accurate powertrain and tire model. there should be road tiles as well, like for the trains. im a fan of trains. there could be grippy road tiles for building racetracks, and less grippy road tiles for building less grippy racetracks, like a rally track, for example. see who can go around the fastest. thats racing! all things considered, this game shows potential, but fails to fully actualize it.
This game is simply amazing and as addictive as a drug. Play at your own risk!

Incredible depth, engineered to perfection. I've watched a sort of 'making of' documentary of this and the amount of work that's gone into it is incredible. My son has played it for countless hours. We love this game.

If your just slightly interested, buy it and bath in the glory of growing your factory. 
Dopamine and shifted time perception garanteed.

Electronic Crack for the Autistic (or g*rman).
Absolute steal for the amount of time you'll invest in the game!
at first, its a tiny bit slow for my liking, but then you start automating ♥♥♥♥ and all the funny chemicals make time go by and now im writing this at 2 am after my first session
this is the best game when it came to automation , logistics and playing it endless , there is no clear end goal , its SOO complicated but i love it

While this is a very good game that I've spent a lot of time on, I highly advise you to review the creator of this game, Kovarex's, Reddit comments. His digital footprint is tainted by heavily "anti-SJW" sentiments, such as referring to "statutory rape" as an "SJW buzzword". Once you're done, decide whether or not you still want to purchase and play this game. If you do, the review below is relevant to you. Otherwise, you may stop reading and move on. I wish I had this luxury when I bought Factorio.

As an automation game, Factorio does its job well. You will spend hundreds of hours building even the most basic of factories. They will not be efficient at first. That comes with time. Depending on who you are, this game can get confusing before long. While not required, it is helpful to have a background in some form of programming and/or software development. This helps you understand the fundamentals of issue tracing, supply function errors, and optimization. Inversely, learning this game may help you develop these skills if you do not have them.

If you are able to stomach the large jump from solid material processing to oil extraction and refinement (yes, that is a HUGE beginner filter), the game will get much more interesting and a whole lot more satisfying.

If you love automation and outpost building, if you love trains, if you love resource gathering, if you love environmental combat and terraforming to meet your needs, this game is for you.
My autistic brain approves...good game all around...numbers go up.
Yes I recommend Crack in game form to everyone , its called Cracktorio

Health warning... this game is seriously addictive. If you are a programmer, stay far and don't think you can handle it. Once you've entered the rabbit hole there is no hope. I've spent about 400h playing just a first game and nowhere near getting to the end. The complexity grows once you play but everything can be fixed with proper blueprint and time.... more and more time. Please be warned. Thank you for your attention in this matter.

do NOT get this game if you are autistic. you WILL sink ALL of your waking hours into this game for months on end. from a health standpoint, this is worse than cigarettes and alcohol. you will not be able to sleep because the factory must continue to grow
Factorio is the blueprint for a factory game, the optimization is insane, great modding community with endless opportunities, the best game of all time.

I can't stop playing it. When I lay down to go to sleep at night, the voices keep whispering to me, and they whisper that the factory must grow. WIth every repeat of them whispering that to me I picture in my head placing down some assemblers, making a belt line, crafting splitters and inserters and bringing everything together, and the sound effect that plays every time you place them, oh god is it beautiful. It puts me in a mental tug of war where I have to choose between sleep or to force myself out of bed to break my brain for the next five hours straight, but I never regret it.
Honestly one of the best games i've played. Amazing Graphics, Fun with Friends and the dlc is just worth it.

I've just barely made it over the line and launched my first rocket, but Factorio is probably the greatest single player experience I've ever had. I'd love to call it best in class but it's really in a league of its own. Never quite played something that engages your problem solving skills to this degree. Incredibly satisfying and worth every cent.

Factorio brings back that old nostalgic feeling I got when I first discovered Minecraft. I watched a lot of EthosLab on YouTube and learned all about redstone. This game is like that but on crack! It takes time to learn. But I suggest you DO NOT look at tutorials online. Just stick to the Factorio Wiki. If you do this, you will have the joy of discovery each time you have an aha moment! But if you do watch tutorials, that's ok too. Maybe just stick to the beginners guides for now and allow yourself to learn the more advanced stuff as you play. GOOD LUCK! This game is definitely in my top 10 favorites of all time! If you're still on the fence, then consider watching a couple of reviews. But if you do decide to buy... say goodbye to your free time... You have been warned!

I'll admit that this game might not be for everybody. However, if it happens to be for you, let me tell you that you are in for a threat! You will never stop learning in that game, you'll always want to optimise something and you'll never go to sleep early ever again. 

Get that game, it is one of a kind!
Absolutely in a class of it's own. An inspiration to others and more content than anyone could possibly work through.

Iteration 38
Planning: Satisfying. Organised. Efficient.
Testing: Output is slow. Input too low. Panic changes.
Mama mia! I made-a spaghetti again.
Iteration 39...

Engineer's dream game. Everyone's solutions differ. Everyone has great ideas... and terrible ones. Everyone struggles with something another person finds easy. Everyone likes different aspects. And so on.

Very cool. Not perfect, but very cool (Gleba needs an easier method to get basic metals).

I'll be honest, Space age is very much for people that have completed the game and it detracts somewhat from the overall experience of new players. 
But this IS the grand daddy of the genre. 
Space age has a learning curve of here is a cliff <shove> too bad too sad if you aren't ready.
I'd recommend dyson sphere project and satisfactory over this for more casual players but there is a demo, and you will know what you are in for by the end of the demo. If you like creating huge bases of sprawling factories turing one component into another this game is for you. 
What it does very well is that you are constantly unlocking new <things> that change how you need to build out your base, 
My kids call it spreadsheet games. But there is a reason I'm still playing years after getting it ,am over 500 hours in and haven't finished the expansion. 
Over all highly positive but if you do end up in a corner and feel like it is just a grind, step back and look at what you are doing. The game will not hold your hand and will let you make mistakes that feel like you want to throw the game away. 
....And yet there is a gem that scratches an itch that can't be found in many clones that came after it.
You will need to look up a guide at some point and that is ok (eg nuclear power ratios) but it is a game that lets you build to a scale that most don't and it doesn't feel like you were robbed or forced to pay to win (there is no pay to win) 20,000,000 green chips is an achievment, and you aren't making these in lots of 10.
You'll think the game sucks and then realise there are tools you unlocked that you ignored that make your current issue easy again. 
It will not hold your hand
After 12,069 hours of play< I totally like this game fun to build and create.
super overpriced but its pretty fun, also this game will run on literally anything
So I did not play much at first, but then i played again, kept playing, and then it was 10 PM.

Cracktorio. I had around 200+ hours sailing the high seas, but had to buy it since it's so good. 
Space Age is also excellent and adds a ton of content, and the devs and mod support are awesome.
Over 100 hours under 2 weeks..... should have just gotten addicted to cracked
Absolute masterpiece, an incredible gem, the unprecedented marvel of quality of life for making the factory grow.

the game is a masterpiece even though i haven't reached space age yet since i started a new savefile when i got space age

also spaghetti is yummy
The fans do not lie when they call it cracktorio. It is an amazing game, its for nerds but it is so fun. space age is also amazing. it slightly increases complexity and maximizises fun.BUY THE DLC!!!

Everything in Factorio is built to be fluid if you do it right, and a brick wall if you don't.
Bang your head on it, eventually it will flow.

Only Cons are:
 - Exponential need for basic resources (iron, copper)
 - Exponential time suck. (No, really)

I despise this game with everything in my body. If you were to write the word "hate" on every thing inside and outside my body down to the plank length it would not be near enough to describe the true anger this game has made my aquire. I thought this  game would be a normal factory game like no other. I was wrong by a mile. I would rather get each individual hair on my head pulled one at a time by a middle aged man than play this horrible game. anyways this game is pretty good.
this game is like minecraft on roids, beware of the hours you will spend figuring out systems thinking... good work

Love it. Originally played Industrialist only on Roblox, but Factorio is such an easy game to get hooked on. So much to do, so much to see, and it's so addictive I love it. It gives you tips to help you through complex mechanics, and various useful QOL keybinds.

Very fun to squeeze as much production in as small of an area as possible. The orchestrated chaos is beautiful. Until your mine runs out...

I will note: This game is significantly more fun with mods that allow you more grace with underground transport and storage. However, this will restrict your achievements to literally none, as most games do with mods.

If you enjoy thinking and planning activities that are common amongst factory games, factorio will scratch that itch. The in depth logic wiring side of the automation is also a welcome change that is very helpful to have access to.

This game is a problem, Every time I launch it I put another 10 hours into the game. This game is crack for nerds.
Smart- 
Declan Kirschbaum
Such a fun game. I feel my logic improving in just the few short hours of playing.
Great game. 200hrs in and I've barely even scratched the surface
Factorio is the peak of Factory gaming. Like. Genuinely this is the ideal of the genre. There is no game better at being a factory game, and there never will be a better factory game than factorio.

The game where solving a neat problem requires you to solve a different neat problem which requires you to solve a different neat problem which requires you to solve a different neat problem which requires you to solve a different neat problem which

I bought this game not knowing much but now I've spent 27 hours and haven't slept well and I Love it.

10/10 would lose sleep again.

As bad as I am at this game, it's so much fun. If you like factory games at all, this is the pinnacle of factory games. And the modding community is alive and VERY well. There's accommodation for those who want to play however they want, which is great for people who want their own experience.

Even after 1177 hours play , this game just keeps me griped there are no set rules on how to layout you supply lines and care must be taken or you can quickly tie yourself in knots. that said this games offers just about all you could wish for in a game i have never come across anything that challenges you in so many ways and just keeps on giving so much to do its mind blowing the enemy must be delt with and taking out whenever you get the chance or you run the risk of been over run and all your hard work will be destroyed. i have not completed the game but have had many attempts i have never grown tied of playing the game. i could go on for ever on how good i think this game is i am not going to do that i will simply say of all the games i have played over the years this is in my opinion the best game i have ever played and i will continue to enjoy it every time i play
I don't play video games that often but this game is DIFFERENT. This game is amazing. Definitely recommend. Its really fun to learn how all of the different things in the game works.
Highly recommended, unless you enjoy being employed, going outside on weekends, and sleeping.

This game scratches an itch that I didn't realize I had. The constant learning and optimizing and building on just the world you start in is immense. Add in the fact that there is now the ability for intersteallar travel, intersteallar logistics, and planets with their own unique traits, resources and rules... and this is a game you can easily dump hundreds of hours into and not be bored. The devs seem to actively care about the game and are tweaking and posting good change logs. This is a game where I blink and somehow 3 hours have elapsed. Like most games, I cannot say this is for everyone, but I have enjoyed the challenge, and I keep coming back. Worth every cent of the price paid for the game.
Its very engaging and addictive try it its worth all the money you can get.

i forsee myself having at least 10 thousand hours by the time i die.

note: DO THE ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ TUTORIAL YOU INCELS.

not only can this game run a decent phone (mmmmmm virtual machine)

it runs well. just remember the limiting factor isnt your imagination. its how much you can abuse your machine.

Best game i ever played, also recommend to research tank. After this one, you will be unstoppable in biter spawner destroying.
Any time i wanted to do something and google it, i met that the game know, that i will need it, but a small problem always is that some tech is hard to research still required right now to build a machine for researching it)
Also, idk why, but logic of vehicle (car, tank, train) movement is strange. In every game i played when you pull arrow down (backwards and brake), car stop and then move back, but here it just stops. Really problem, when you drive around biter spawners and throw grenades from a car, because one rock and you leave the car right where stopped and most times die with it. I believe, that i just didn't recognize, how to configure car movement logic, but there's no such setting, or i'm bad in searching. If it's configurable or will be fixed in next version, it will be best game i played without minuses)

One of the greatest video games ever made. 

Completely overwhelming in scope to learn, but incredibly satisfying when you do.
Its the factory game and its good.  If you like constant puzzles and solving supply issues this is the game for you

One of the rare games that is really REALLY well done. 
Most functions that you might think of are implemented in the game. Performance is great.
Gameplay is also super fun. There are tons of different mechanics that are slowly unlocked during a playthrough. Some mechanics might be easily ignored to "win" and can later be mastered during next playthroughs. 
Maps are flexibly configurable: aliens, resources, biomes, etc.
Also, there are some scenarios when the freeplay becomes old.
10/10

Incredible game, very addicting.
The factory must grow
almost made me fail calculus 3 and control systems in college. It is the greatest video game that I can not recommend out of almost failing classes, it is just that addicting.

This is THE factory game. If you want a chill game just about building the best factories possible, its got that. If you want super difficult and high tension cerebral gameplay, its got that too.
You can play with friends. If you enjoy this kind of thing this is the OG.

[h1]The factory must grow... and yet it yields results.[/h1]

It could be seen as an analogy for how human beings are imperfect and incomplete, needing to grow and perfect, and yet are able to generate something good (or valuable) for the world.

Objectivelly, great game, basically an open-world puzzle game with wild replayability and value for your money. Clear gameplay loop, lots of quality of life and a several solutions for each problem. If you like optimizing stuff and finding solutions for problems, this game is for you. 

Also, very low required specs: played it on a intel HD graphics 620 without major issues. Don't wait for a sale, there are none. If in doubt, play the demo, it is very generous.
This is a masterclass of software engineering and game design, fun as hell. I have high hopes and good wishes for Wube as they move on to other projects.

i swear every time i start the game, it turns dark outside and i'm missing several hours of my day. It can get complicated and almost overwhelming when you realize you need to relocate some materials from the middle of your factory, all the way across to your oil refinery or something. Just take your time, it'll work out and you can always rebuild - it's not as stressful or daunting as it seems

This game made me feel smart until my friends joined my server and told me everything I did wrong, and long story short I spent 40 hours trying to get better and I don't plan on stopping anytime soon.

Wanted to play factorio for a bit.
Why its 5am now?

Best game, if you want to travel to future while having fun. XD
Factorio. Fabulous game, very captivating if factory automation games are your thing.

I was recommended to play Factorio by Reddit. This game is extremely addictive. It feels like a puzzle game, but honestly, the game allows you to have flexibility in how you play it. For instance, you can decide yourself how you set up your "factory", whether that's doing it intuitively or creating a whole advanced spreadsheet to assist you. Very fun to play co-op
certainly the numbers must be wrong. no way i spend almost every seconds of 4 month of my life playing this game... in the steam version only.
I thought they were joking when they said this game is like crack cocaine for autistic people...
its fine....it stopped me from eating becuase i forgot i was hungry because the factory must grow
Favorite game with most hours on it there is really much content and a ton of mods my favorite part is when you just automate something and you go like oh wait i dont need to do that anymore yay
basic top down two dee factory builder with walls and turrets fun but be aware this game will confuse you if you don't have a head for computers at higher tech levels of the game.
I only got this about a month ago and not 5 years ago for some reason and it runs perfectly on my ♥♥♥♥ laptop
Fantastic game! Will play the expansion and space stuff after I get all the og achievements!

Best game in the factory builder genre. Not as pretty as Satisfactory but has more solid gameplay and much better late-game scaling. Replayability is fantastic, Space Age completely refreshes the game and beyond that there are many popular modpacks that completely revamp how the game works.

It's a lot of fun! Definitely don't be afraid to look up guides and help because it's complicated. But it's fun to face challenges and try to solve them, especially when you turn enemies off so you can look at it as a more pure infrastructure challenge. As I get more comfortable with it, I'll turn enemies on (one day lol).
What an addicting Factory management game. From optimizing production lines, to obliterating aliens with drive by rockets. Gosh I love this game
One of the best factory building games on Steam, and is worth buying.

[PL] Świetna i wciągająca gra o automatyzacji.
[EN] Great and addictive game about automation.
Extremely addicting.  I havn't played it much on PC but on my switch I have over 300 hours. I suggest to anyone and everyone to play this amazing game and above all else... THE FACTORY MUST GROW!

I highly recommend this title to anyone who enjoys factory building and resource management. It was well worth the $12 for the Base Game that alone got me 100 Hours of content (I will confess I might just be bad at the game). The Space Age DLC is also worth the money because it adds a LOT of content to the game. There is a immense amount of mods for the game so even if you beat the Base Game, and DLC there are plenty of modpacks to keep you playing like Krastorio 2, or Space Exploration.

9/10 Experience, Highly Recommended, a sort of 'Forever Game' for me.

Awesome game. It's one of those games where I've played it for 250 hours and technically I'm not a beginner, but I also feel like I've only scratched the surface of what is possible in this game. All of the different systems have so much depth that you could probably spend hundreds of hours doing circuits or trains and still learn new things.

Playing Factorio helped me get through some darker times. I know it's a distraction, but sometimes that's exactly what you need and Factorio is a great game if you want to give your mind a break and just focus on automating production lines.

You can play it for 15 minutes, you can play it for several hours. You can build rockets and go to space or you can optimize unloading a train for one of your factories and that's what is great about this game - you can just play it the way you want to play it.

The factory must grow.

Command & Conquer meets Sim City in this addicting masterpiece of a game. The learning curve is steep and the ongoing frustration is real, but I'm 100 hours in and I don't have an once of me that want's to stop playing anytime soon. 

Highly recommended.
this game is super dificult and verry long but you will love it in the end if u like it or not
If you like engineering or software design, this game will keep you playing for hours on end. Very fun

It is simple but complex, enables / allows for creativity but still has structure. It is a game about creating things and being positive but you do need to be strong and kill enemies and defend yourself.

Well optimized for performance. Good engineering. All engineers should try it.
And it looks and sounds good, too. Such an art.
GOAT of automation games.
Too good, the graphics look deceiving for how amazing this game gets.
I avoided this one for so long and that was a huge mistake. Very fun and addicting, probably my favorite factory game.

Over 100 hours into this game and I still don't understand how the worms make such amazing predictions from 200 meters away.

Nice game.

A game that makes you use your brain, solve problems and approach situations in interesting and rewarding ways

A lot of games generate worlds that often end up being boring after a few hours, but this feels like there's so much to upgrade, so much to optimise and learn and improve

Definitely will be playing more of this!
This is game is very good and it is a problem. I have not seen sunlight in 3 weeks and have forgotten what grass looks like. If you never want to see your family again buy this game.

i make love to this game. i think i have a copy on every device and OS!
